audit_items.proof_trace: Every pointer dereference in ppolicy.c is guarded: the BerElement pointer is runtime null-checked after ber_init() (L145-148); optional output parameters (expirep, gracep, errorp, secondsp) are guarded by explicit runtime null checks; the server-controlled bv_val is checked at L137 before use; and ld/ctrl are API-contract parameters protected by assert(), which accurately documents that external LDAP protocol data controls only the BER payload content, not the validity of these handles themselves.
